hypothesis: If a chemically informed autoencoder combined with a conditional latent diffusion model (CompGen), pretrained on OC20 and fine-tuned on HEA, is used to generate shell-wise surface compositions conditioned on adsorbate identity and adsorption energy, then it will outperform chemically-agnostic baselines by producing compositions that more closely match target distributions (lower Fréchet Distance), better satisfy element constraints (lower Leakage), and have higher point-wise similarity to ground truth (higher Cosine Similarity), while maintaining performance under reduced fine-tuning data.
